summary: The Check Changelog Entry job failed because the workflow determined the PR targets v1.16 but there is no changelog entry in .changes/v1.16 (the check reported a missing changelog entry).
root_cause: The changelog validation script determined there are no changed .changes files for the expected target version (v1.16) and failed the job because the PR is missing a changelog entry for .changes/v1.16.

evidence.quote: ##[error]Currently this PR would target a v1.16 release. Please add a changelog entry for in the .changes/v1.16 folder, or discuss which release you'd like to target with your reviewer. If you believe this change does not need a changelog entry, please add the 'no-changelog-needed' label.
evidence.reason: This is the explicit failing error emitted by the job explaining the reason for failure (missing changelog entry for v1.16).
The script set changesPresent to false, indicating it detected no .changes entries in the PR.
The script's changedChangesFiles array is empty, supporting that no changelog files were changed in the PR.
The script reads version/VERSION to compute the expected target version (used to determine the required .changes/v<version> folder).
The action configuration shows which paths/files the check considers (.changes/*, CHANGELOG.md, version/VERSION), confirming the check's expected locations for changelog and version files.

suggested_fix: Add a changelog entry in the .changes/v1.16 folder for this PR (or, if appropriate, add the 'no-changelog-needed' label to the PR).

summary: Dependabot updater exited with an error because it determined no security update was needed for the dependency 'rand' (security-only run), and the updater returned a non-zero exit code.
root_cause: Dependabot reported a 'security_update_not_needed' error for dependency 'rand' during a security-only update and the updater process exited with code 1.

evidence.quote: no security update needed as rand is no longer vulnerable
evidence.reason: Shows Dependabot determined that the dependency 'rand' does not need a security update.
The summary errors table explicitly lists a 'security_update_not_needed' error type.

evidence.quote: Error: Command failed with exit code 1: /bin/sh -c $DEPENDABOT_HOME/dependabot-updater/bin/run update_files
evidence.reason: The updater process terminated with a non-zero exit code, causing the container/job to fail.
The job definition shows this was a security-only update run, which explains why Dependabot aborted when no security fix was needed.

evidence.quote: For more information see: https://github.com/rust-lang/cargo/network/updates/1333710739 (write access to the repository is required to view the log)
evidence.reason: Indicates additional logs are gated by repository write access, which may be needed to inspect full details of the updater run.
Confirms the updater reported an error count and the job reported failure.

suggested_fix: No action is required if the dependency is indeed no longer vulnerable. If you expected an update, run a non-security update (set "security-updates-only" to false) or update the dependency version manually. To inspect full updater logs, grant the runner/Dependabot the repository write access noted in the error message so you can view the detailed log at the provided URL.

metadata.provider_error: RuntimeError: diagnosis command exited 1: diagnosis_shim_openai: JSONDecodeError: Expecting value: line 19 column 16 (char 1087)

summary: The job failed when attempting to push a branch to GitHub; the git push was rejected with a 403 permission error for the nextjs-bot account.
root_cause: git push failed because GitHub returned a permission error: "remote: Permission to vercel/next.js.git denied to nextjs-bot." (HTTP 403) when running 'git push origin update/react/19.3.0-canary-142cfde8-20260422'.

evidence.quote: Error: Command failed with exit code 128 (Unknown system error -128): git push origin update/react/19.3.0-canary-142cfde8-20260422
evidence.reason: Shows the failing command was a git push to the update/react branch.

evidence.quote: remote: Permission to vercel/next.js.git denied to nextjs-bot.
evidence.reason: Explicit GitHub permission denial indicating the authenticated identity (nextjs-bot) does not have push access.

evidence.quote: "fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/vercel/next.js/': The requested URL returned error: 403"
evidence.reason: HTTP 403 from GitHub confirms the push was blocked due to lack of permission.
The job sets the git user to nextjs-bot, matching the identity reported in the permission error.
A token is present in the job environment; the failure suggests the credentials used for push are not authorized for the operation.
Stack trace points to the sync script performing the git push that failed.

suggested_fix: Ensure the credentials used for git push have write access to vercel/next.js. Concretely: verify the job is using a token with repo/push permissions (for example the workflow's GITHUB_TOKEN with appropriate write scopes or a PAT with repo scope), confirm the token is actually used for the git remote (e.g. set remote URL to include the token or configure git to use the ACTIONS-provided token), or grant the nextjs-bot account push access to the repository. Re-run the sync after fixing the credential/permission configuration.

summary: A single fourslash test failed due to an assertion mismatch: the code-fix description produced by the compiler did not match the expected text in the test file.
root_cause: The fourslash test at tests/cases/fourslash/codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts failed because an assertion expected a different code-fix message text than was produced (mismatch between 'Add return type \'Foo<string>\'' and the longer expected text).

evidence.reason: Shows there was a failing test in the run.
Identifies the failing test suite as the fourslash tests.
The exact test file that failed is printed in the log.

evidence.quote: AssertionError: expected 'Add return type \'Foo<string>\'' to equal 'Add return type \'Foo<string, string[…'
evidence.reason: The assertion failure shows the produced code-fix message ('Add return type \'Foo<string>\'') did not match the expected message (the longer 'Add return type \'Foo<string, string[…').
The command used to run tests (useful for reproducing the failure locally).

suggested_fix: Either update the expected code-fix description text in tests/cases/fourslash/codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts to match the actual message 'Add return type \'Foo<string>\'', or change the code-fix implementation to produce the originally expected message. Re-run the test suite locally with: npm test -- --no-lint --coverage to verify the fix.
